summary: The article supports measurement-related mechanism claims about why visible repository traces miss some coding-agent use, while prevalence and group-difference claims remain bounded rather than causally explained.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The study estimates coding-agent adoption on GitHub at 15.85% to 22.60% across 129,134 projects.
bounded_claims.bounds: This is a heuristic prevalence estimate rather than a causal explanation, and it is bounded by the study's detectable trace design rather than direct observation of all coding-agent use.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Coding-agent adoption was higher in newer projects and somewhat higher in large organizations than in the overall file-level baseline.
bounded_claims.bounds: The evidence shows descriptive distributional differences only; it does not establish why younger repositories or large organizations show higher adoption, and the comparisons remain bounded by file-level measurement.
